name: Broadcast
description: Jellyfin Broadcast generates a continuous programming schedule from your media library and exposes it as a live channel with M3U/XMLTV support.
overview: Turns your Jellyfin library into a single always-on TV channel

versions.changelog: Fix: regenerating the schedule (daily task, library changes, config saves) advanced each block's Active Series and inflated movie airing counts by a full schedule's worth every time, and cut off whatever was currently playing. Active Series/Cooldown history is now committed from what actually aired, and the currently-playing program is preserved across regeneration. Fix: a full library query ran per scheduled program instead of once per block, and movie history was queried per candidate per pick instead of once per run -- both are now cached/batched. Fix: a DST spring-forward could crash regeneration; overlapping Programming Blocks are now rejected at validation; the XMLTV guide was missing whatever's airing right now. Security: manual Regenerate now requires elevated privileges; the config page no longer embeds the admin's session token in the M3U/XMLTV/Stream URLs. Config page: block fields are now shown/hidden based on content type and ordering.

Fix: plugin was disabled at startup because it referenced Jellyfin.Controller/Jellyfin.Model 10.11.11 while the target server runs 10.11.8 -- Jellyfin's plugin loader binds these by exact assembly version with no redirects. Now pinned to 10.11.8. Also fixed the earlier Windows-native e_sqlite3.dll packaging issue.

Fix: plugin was disabled at startup (Malfunctioned) because Jellyfin's plugin loader tries to load every *.dll under the plugin folder as a managed assembly, including the bundled Windows-native e_sqlite3.dll. That native binary is no longer packaged. Also: config page now shows ready-to-copy M3U/XMLTV/Stream URLs.

Fix: plugin failed to load ("NotSupported") because Microsoft.Data.Sqlite and its native SQLite dependencies weren't packaged alongside the plugin DLL. Also: config page now shows ready-to-copy M3U/XMLTV/Stream URLs (with API key included) for adding the channel to Jellyfin's own Live TV.
Config page now shows ready-to-copy M3U/XMLTV/Stream URLs (with API key included) for adding the channel to Jellyfin's own Live TV as a tuner + guide.
